summary: Shaw uses an extended satirical dialogue to criticize open source software projects that cater to difficult community members at the expense of regular users. The allegory portrays FLOSS maintainers as bakers who put 'turds' (bad design decisions) in free cookies to appease a loud, obnoxious 'sentient fly' (toxic power users), while charging others for the clean version. The piece skewers the defensive posture maintainers take when receiving criticism, hiding behind the 'it's free' defense and bureaucratic contribution processes. Shaw argues that offering something for free doesn't immunize it from criticism, and that prioritizing toxic community members over ordinary users is self-destructive. The story ends with the café empty except for the fly—illustrating how this dynamic drives away the broader user base.
key_points: Open source projects often make design concessions to appease loud, toxic community members rather than serving the majority of users
The 'it was free' defense is used to deflect legitimate criticism of poor software decisions
Contribution processes (bug trackers, forms, codes of conduct) are weaponized to silence critics rather than improve the product
Maintainers conflate worship and gratitude with genuine user feedback, prioritizing ego over quality
The end result of catering to toxic minorities is an empty restaurant—a project that drove away its actual user base
money_quotes: You put free cookies out with turds in them because a sentient fly demands turds, and then you charge people for cookies without turds? That's fucking insane.
You offered them to me asshole! If I'd known there was turds in the free cookies I would have just bought the fucking turdless cookies from you.
No, that's destructive because it is telling me to change my mind about a clearly obvious and brilliant decision I made to put turds in my cookies.
All patrons must submit orders through our RTFMS and we handle only the orders we feel are worth our time.
If I give you free cookies you have to worship me! Worship me for my free cookies asshole!
The truth comes out! I have to worship you and it's not about having the best cookies in town. It's about you being recognized by giant sentient flies and nobody else.
Probably because you suck so bad nobody would pay you to do it for real.
key_insight: Open source projects that prioritize appeasing their loudest toxic users over serving the broader community end up with an empty room and a product nobody else wants to use.
